key_insight: Local short-range ordering in grain-boundary complexions significantly affects the mechanical properties of nanocrystalline Al-Ni alloys.
engineering_relevance: Tailoring grain-boundary complexion can optimize mechanical properties for applications in nanocrystalline materials.
method: Employing large-scale Monte Carlo and molecular dynamics simulations.
Examining effects at two Ni concentrations (2 at.% and 4 at.%).
Analyzing mechanical responses post-annealing at different temperatures (913 K and 378 K).
evidence_signal: The study reveals that amorphous complexions suppressed shear localization, while SRO complexions resulted in higher strength but led to heterogeneous stress concentrations.
watch_item: The complex interactions between grain-boundary states and mechanical responses may vary with additional alloying elements.
paper_title: Amorphous vs. Short-Range-Ordered Complexions: Consequences for Grain-Boundary-Mediated Plasticity in Nanocrystalline Al-Ni Alloys
